break_glass.description:
BreakGlass is true when the identity's recovery address has break-glass
recovery enabled for the identity's current organization.

source.description:
Whether the member has access through the project directly or through
workspace membership.

schema.description: Error responses are sent when an error (e.g. unauthorized, bad request, ...) occurred.

debug.description:
Debug information

This field is often not exposed to protect against leaking
sensitive information.
debug.example: SQL field "foo" is not a bool.

debug.description: Debug contains debug information. This is usually not available and has to be enabled.
debug.example: The database adapter was unable to find the element

error.example: The requested resource could not be found

error_description.description: Description contains further information on the nature of the error.
error_description.example: Object with ID 12345 does not exist

id.description:
ID is a unique error ID.
feature_not_available ErrFeatureNotAvailable
quota_exceeded ErrQuotaExceeded

status_code.description: Code represents the error status code (404, 403, 401, ...).

id.description:
The error ID

Useful when trying to identify various errors in application logic.

message.example: The resource could not be found

reason.description: A human-readable reason for the error
reason.example: User with ID 1234 does not exist.

request.description:
The request ID

The request ID is often exposed internally in order to trace
errors across service architectures. This is often a UUID.

schema.title: Error response
